etymology.description: ه›—(ه›—) (wأ©i, "enclosure") + ç•گ(ç•گ) (fأ؛, "abundant") = grain stored in an enclosure -> storing grains
etymology.interpretation: The character ه›·(jأ¹n, "storing grains") combines ه›—(ه›—) (wأ©i, "enclosure"), indicating a confined space where items are held, with ç•گ(ç•گ) (fأ؛, "abundant"), suggesting a surplus of grain being kept safe and protected. This combination effectively conveys the idea of storing grains securely.
